마태복음 20:1.content: “For the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who was the master of a household, who went out early in the morning to hire laborers for his vineyard.

마태복음 20:2.content: When he had agreed with the laborers for a denarius a day, he sent them into his vineyard.

마태복음 20:3.content: He went out about the third hour, and saw others standing idle in the marketplace.

마태복음 20:4.content: He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and whatever is right I will give you.’ So they went their way.

마태복음 20:5.content: Again he went out about the sixth and the ninth hour, and did likewise.

마태복음 20:6.content: About the eleventh hour he went out, and found others standing idle. He said to them, ‘Why do you stand here all day idle?’

마태복음 20:7.content: “They said to him, ‘Because no one has hired us.’ “He said to them, ‘You also go into the vineyard, and you will receive whatever is right.’

마태복음 20:8.content: When evening had come, the lord of the vineyard said to his manager, ‘Call the laborers and pay them their wages, beginning from the last to the first.’

마태복음 20:9.content: “When those who were hired at about the eleventh hour came, they each received a denarius.

마태복음 20:10.content: When the first came, they supposed that they would receive more; and they likewise each received a denarius.

마태복음 20:11.content: When they received it, they murmured against the master of the household,

마태복음 20:12.content: saying, ‘These last have spent one hour, and you have made them equal to us, who have borne the burden of the day and the scorching heat!’

마태복음 20:13.content: “But he answered one of them, ‘Friend, I am doing you no wrong. Didn’t you agree with me for a denarius?

마태복음 20:14.content: Take that which is yours, and go your way. It is my desire to give to this last just as much as to you.

마태복음 20:15.content: Isn’t it lawful for me to do what I want to with what I own? Or is your eye evil, because I am good?’

마태복음 20:16.content: So the last will be first, and the first last. For many are called, but few are chosen.”

마태복음 20:17.content: As Jesus was going up to Jerusalem, he took the twelve disciples aside, and on the way he said to them,

마태복음 20:18.content: “Behold, we are going up to Jerusalem, and the Son of Man will be delivered to the chief priests and scribes, and they will condemn him to death,

마태복음 20:19.content: and will hand him over to the Gentiles to mock, to scourge, and to crucify; and the third day he will be raised up.”

마태복음 20:20.content: Then the mother of the sons of Zebedee came to him with her sons, kneeling and asking a certain thing of him.

마태복음 20:21.content: He said to her, “What do you want?” She said to him, “Command that these, my two sons, may sit, one on your right hand, and one on your left hand, in your Kingdom.”

마태복음 20:22.content: But Jesus answered, “You don’t know what you are asking. Are you able to drink the cup that I am about to drink, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with?” They said to him, “We are able.”

마태복음 20:23.content: He said to them, “You will indeed drink my cup, and be baptized with the baptism that I am baptized with, but to sit on my right hand and on my left hand is not mine to give; but it is for whom it has been prepared by my Father.”

마태복음 20:24.content: When the ten heard it, they were indignant with the two brothers.

마태복음 20:25.content: But Jesus summoned them, and said, “You know that the rulers of the nations lord it over them, and their great ones exercise authority over them.

마태복음 20:26.content: It shall not be so among you, but whoever desires to become great among you shall be your servant.

마태복음 20:27.content: Whoever desires to be first among you shall be your bondservant,

마태복음 20:28.content: even as the Son of Man came not to be served, but to serve, and to give his life as a ransom for many.”

마태복음 20:29.content: As they went out from Jericho, a great multitude followed him.

마태복음 20:30.content: Behold, two blind men sitting by the road, when they heard that Jesus was passing by, cried out, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”

마태복음 20:31.content: The multitude rebuked them, telling them that they should be quiet, but they cried out even more, “Lord, have mercy on us, you son of David!”

마태복음 20:32.content: Jesus stood still, and called them, and asked, “What do you want me to do for you?”

마태복음 20:33.content: They told him, “Lord, that our eyes may be opened.”

마태복음 20:34.content: Jesus, being moved with compassion, touched their eyes; and immediately their eyes received their sight, and they followed him.
